title: How Anthropic Employees ACTUALLY Use Claude Code to Grow
summary: Austin Marchese analyzes how Anthropic's Growth Team leveraged Claude Code to scale from $1 billion to $19 billion in 14 months. The video outlines four key strategies: automating existing workflows, making sub-threshold work viable, building custom tools, and using AI as a thought partner.
writeup: A breakdown of four strategies Anthropic's growth team uses with Claude Code, drawn from blog posts, podcasts, and internal videos featuring head of growth Amol Avaisar and growth lead Austin Lau. The through-line is that AI does not merely speed up existing work: it unlocks a new category of tasks that were previously abandoned because the manual cost was too high. Concrete examples include a Figma plugin that cut ad-variation time from 30 minutes to 30 seconds, an automated growth-experimentation system called CASH (Claude Accelerates Sustainable Hypergrowth), and a manager-simulation skill built from public writing and Slack history.

similar.title: How Anthropic Engineers ACTUALLY Prompt Claude Code
How Anthropic Doesn't Build the WRONG Things with Claude
What Karpathy Joining Anthropic Actually Means For Claude
I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
Top 6 Claude Code Features to Get You Ahead of 99% of Users. (In Under 10 Minutes).

key_points: Austin Lau, a non-technical growth lead who had to Google how to open a terminal when Claude Code launched, built a Figma plugin in roughly one hour that generates ad variations with a single click, cutting per-batch ad creation from 30 minutes to 30 seconds and achieving a claimed 10x creative output.

Anthropic's growth team allocates time 70% toward large-swing experiments and only 30% toward small optimizations, inverting what Amol describes as the traditional 60-70% small-to-medium, 20-30% large-swing split that most growth teams use.

The CASH program (Claude Accelerates Sustainable Hypergrowth) hands the entire growth-experimentation loop to AI: Claude identifies opportunities, builds the experiment, tests it, and analyzes results, running experiments the team would never have had bandwidth to do manually.

Lau built a Google Ads API plugin that lets Claude query live campaign data and apply budget changes from a phone without opening a laptop, a tool hyper-specific to his role that no one else at Anthropic uses, illustrating the 'build for yourself' principle.

Amol built a manager-simulation skill by feeding Claude his manager's public writing, their Slack conversations, and her frameworks, then asking it weekly what feedback she would give based on everything he had or had not done that week.

Amol's AI thought-partner setup includes a scheduled co-worker agent that scans roughly 25 charts each morning and surfaces which metrics need attention and which show concerning signals, so he arrives to a prioritized digest rather than raw dashboards.
